title: task
description: slip.rs:177-183 - The TX ring buffer only resets tx_write when the queue is completely empty. If packets are queued and dequeued without fully emptying the queue, tx_write grows monotonically until it hits TX_BUFFER_SIZE, causing PacketTooLarge errors even when most of the buffer is unused. Failure scenario: Gateway under steady load queues and dequeues packets but never fully empties queue. After ~4KB of cumulative traffic, all new packets are rejected with PacketTooLarge even though actual queue depth is low. Fix: Compact buffer when dequeuing, or use a true ring buffer with wrap-around.
close_reason: Fixed: TX ring buffer now uses head/tail pointers with proper wrap-around, reusing consumed space even while packets pending
